title: can you give me an overview of Prez's last raid? give some areas of improvements

turns.userText: can you give me an overview of Prez's last raid? give some areas of improvements
turns.agentText:
Prez Ashton's last raid was **June 8, 2026** (run `20260608-173500-zosq`). Let me pull the summary and Prez's commander stats in parallel.

Got the data. Let me pull a chart on squad survivability — that's where the clearest improvement signal is — then I'll give you the full breakdown.

Here's the full breakdown of Prez's **June 8 run** (27 fights · 20W / 7L · 30v~54).

---

**Run at a glance**
Prez's squad went 20–7 (74% win rate) while consistently outnumbered ~1.8× — a genuinely strong result. Kill efficiency was solid at a **2.28 KDR** (395 enemy deaths vs 173 squad deaths). Standout performers: **FatherHyperion** (Tempest, 10.6M dmg), **Arx** (Druid — top healing at 6.06M *and* top cleanses at 3,543 while dying only twice), and **MaximDeathgun** (Reaper, 1,939 strips).

---

**Areas for improvement**

{{figure}}

**1 · Bouzouki (Specter) — critical survivability problem**
10 deaths and 20 downs — worst on the squad by a wide margin. A Specter is a support/barrier role; every death is a gap in barrier and healing coverage. 10 deaths across 27 fights (~1 death per 2.7 fights) is unsustainable. This needs a direct conversation about positioning and knowing when to disengage.

**2 · hotep & Ashtonlightstone — extreme down counts**
Both went down 19× despite relatively modest death counts (5 each). They're getting rezzed off the ground constantly, which burns support resources and disrupts cleave. High down counts like this usually point to overextending or not respecting the fallback call.

**3 · IronOre (Druid) — healer dying too often**
9 deaths, 9 downs — and note that downs = deaths, meaning the squad couldn't rally them off the ground at all. Losing a healer on 9 of 27 fights is a real drag on the support core, especially when Sunny (Druid) also posted 6 deaths.

**4 · Firebrand cleanse output is uneven**
Arx and IronOre carried the cleanse load on Druid. But several Firebrands massively underperformed on cleanses: **sbr** (129), **undiee** (309), **Vettexl** (300). With 3 Druids already handling a lot, FBs should be topping up with Mantra of Lore — 27 fights at 30v54 is a conditions-heavy environment.

**5 · Zero/near-zero contributors**
**red armadillo.4593** (Firebrand) posted *exactly* zero across every metric — damage, healing, cleanses, deaths, everything. **Artemis Y.5693** had 13,808 total damage and 11,151 healing over the whole run. Both are almost certainly a disconnect or very late join; worth a quick check-in with them to confirm.

**6 · Overall down rate**
365 squad downs across 27 fights = ~13.5 downs per fight on 30 players. That's roughly 45% of the squad hitting the ground each engagement. Even accounting for the numbers disadvantage, this suggests boon uptime (stability in particular) has room to improve — worth watching across multiple runs to confirm the pattern.

---

**Bottom line:** The win rate and KDR against those numbers are genuinely respectable. The clearest gains are in support survivability (Bouzouki especially), reducing the chronic downtime of hotep/Ashtonlightstone, and evening out cleanse distribution across the Firebrand corps.

turns.userText: if you look in comps you can see our comps and get an idea of the roles of each class
turns.agentText:
No presets in AxiTools — but you may be referring to **AxiForge** comps (your local editor), which is a separate store. Let me check there.

Good — 7 comps in AxiForge. The most relevant ones look like **EWW VoE Comp 4** and **EWW - 4/15 Comp**. Let me read both at once.

The comps reference builds by ID — I need to pull the actual builds to see professions and roles. Let me fetch all unique ones from both comps in parallel.

Now I have the full picture. Let me cross-reference the run data against what each role is actually supposed to be doing.

---

**Role map from your comps (4/15 Comp — current standard)**

Each party of 5 runs: **Support Troubadour** (primary stab) + **Wanderer's Firebrand** (secondary stab) + **Heal Druid** (primary healer/cleanser) + **FAFO Reaper** (DPS + strips) + **Berserker or Untamed** (DPS). The VoE comp swaps in Luminary, Support Harbinger, Catalyst, and Power Scourge in some slots.

---

**Revisited improvement areas — with role context**

**1 · Troubadour positioning: hotep is a clear outlier**
Your comp guide is explicit — Troubadour is *"one of the tankiest roles in the squad"* and should *"stack on commander as much as you can."* Look at the contrast within the same role on the same run:

| Troubadour | Deaths | Downs |
|---|---|---|
| Beornin | 3 | 3 |
| Oceanomega | 3 | 10 |
| WindNature | 4 | 8 |
| moxic | 4 | 7 |
| Yesmad | 5 | 11 |
| **hotep** | **5** | **19** |

Beornin ran the same build and posted 3 downs all night. hotep went down 19 times. This is not a build or role problem — it's hotep specifically overextending or not stacking on tag. Worth a direct conversation.

**2 · Firebrand cleanses: several FBs not using Tome of Resolve**
Your FB guide explicitly calls out: *"If there are heavy condis on party members, you can use Tome of Resolve → Chapter 2: Radiant Recovery for additional cleanses."* In a 30v54 fight, that's the exact scenario where this matters. Yet:

| Firebrand | Cleanses |
|---|---|
| Subvert | 689 |
| Ashtonlightstone | 474 |
| undiee | 309 |
| Vettexl | 300 |
| sbr | 129 |

sbr (129) and Vettexl (300) in particular look like they're not opening Tome of Resolve at all during heavy condi waves. The Druids shouldn't be carrying the entire cleanse burden — FBs need to pitch in more.

**3 · IronOre (Druid): 9 deaths in Minstrel's is a positioning red flag**
Druids in full Minstrel's have the toughness to tank a significant amount of punishment. 9 deaths across 27 fights — nearly 1 every 3 engagements — strongly suggests IronOre is not staying center of stack as the guide instructs. Compare to Arx (same Druid build, 2 deaths). The survival gap between these two players is too large to be explained by anything other than positioning discipline.

**4 · Bouzouki (Specter): the most damaging survivability failure**
Bouzouki had 10 deaths and 20 downs — the worst numbers on the squad — in what is a barrier/healer support role. Every death is a window where their party's barrier coverage drops entirely. The Specter wasn't in your AxiForge comps, so it may be a flex pick, but regardless of comp slot, a support losing 10 fights means their party is regularly going into engagements without barrier coverage.

**5 · Stability chain: Troubadour + FB coordination likely breaking down**
Your Troubadour guide stresses: *"Do not burn all your stab skills at once — space them out. You and your Firebrand should cover gaps between each other."* With 5 of 6 Troubadours posting elevated down counts and several FBs also going down frequently, what you're likely seeing is stab chains collapsing simultaneously rather than being staggered. The result: an entire party goes down in the same spike. The squad's 365 total downs in 27 fights points directly at this — moments where stability coverage has a gap and a whole party eats the burst.

---

**Bottom line for Prez:** The win rate and KDR are genuinely good given the numbers disadvantage. The biggest gains are in **getting hotep to stack**, **getting FBs to use Tome of Resolve actively**, **getting IronOre to stay center**, and **fixing Bouzouki's positioning**. The stability coordination issue is more of a squad-wide coaching point worth addressing in the next pre-raid talk.

f231af41-8473-4921-af2f-a7be1156ed88.notes:
# Heal Druid Guide

---

## General Description:
**Role**: Heals, Cleanse, Resurrect, Damage Mitigation
**Responsibilities**: Druids are the primary healer and cleanser. They provide resurrect utility through their elite skill and additional damage mitigation through their pets.
**Positioning**: Stay close to the center of stack. With full minstrels gear, you have high toughness and a pet that mitigates damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:
- When the commander calls for might stacks, use @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] and @[skill:71963:Oaken Cudgel] to blast fire fields for might.
- If you notice your party line does not have swiftness, you can also use @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] to provide swiftness.
- Right before melee engage/cross movement, cast @[skill:31700:Vine Surge] to catch enemy zerg with immob. You can also throw it into where the enemy zerg is veiling to catch stragglers.
- If people are getting caught with immobs during initial push, you can also cast @[skill:12497:Frost Spirit]  to provide early resistance.
- Enter @[skill:31869:Celestial Avatar] (CA) on clash.

---

## Rotation:
- When in CA, spam @[skill:31796:Cosmic Ray] to heal. **DO NOT USE @[skill:32253:Rejuvenating Tides]**. 
- Use @[skill:32242:Seed of Life] and @[skill:31318:Lunar Impact] for cleanses and @[relic:101268:Relic of Karakosa] proc for heals.
- Use @[skill:12489:Healing Spring] on initial melee clashes. It provides heals, cleanses, and regeneration.
- Outside of CA, rotation through your weapon skills to provide enough heals to return to CA. Skills to use include:
  - @[skill:31496:Sublime Conversion] + @[skill:31535:Ancestral Grace] - Provides a water field and blast finisher, lots of heals.
  - @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] , @[skill:71963:Oaken Cudgel] (This is a movement skill, be careful when using), @[skill:71999:Flourish]
  - @[skill:12620:Hunter's Call] procs @[trait:964:Windborne Notes] , which provides regen
  - Rotating through the above should be more than enough to get back into CA.
- Back to spamming @[skill:31796:Cosmic Ray] 

---

## Tips & Tricks:
- Use @[skill:31700:Vine Surge]  on wells calls or when trying to catch enemy tail. Enemies without resistance will be caught with immob. 
- Cast @[skill:12569:Spirit of Nature]  on downed allies based on your party order or as directed by the commander.
  - <u>Note: there is a decent cast time on the skill so you will need to cast it preemptively.</u> Trying to use spirit on downed allies after they’ve been downed will likely result in them being cleaved out before they can get resurrected. This is especially true on the initial clash where the damage is the highest.
- Use pet skills on CD to proc @[trait:1055:Rejuvenation] . Swap pets when one's dead.

---

# Wanderer's Firebrand Guide

---

## General Description
**Role**: Secondary Stab, Boons, Cleave
**Responsibilities**: Firebrands are the secondary stability provider for the squad. They provide various boons including aegis, resistance, swiftness, quickness, might, etc. and stunbreaks through their elite mantra.
**Positioning**: Stack on commander. With consistent aegis application and a decent amount of toughness (Wanderer’s), firebrands should be tanking a significant amount of incoming damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:

- Charge your mantras the moment you load in/respawn.
- As one of the two stability sources in your party, we encourage you to talk with your Troubadour in your line to discuss who does the first source of stab.
- Ensure your party has swiftness. Use @[skill:9084:"Advance!"]  It is the FB’s responsibility to provide swiftness pre-engage. 
- When commander calls for might stacks, use @[skill:44364:Tome of Justice] -> @[skill:40015:Chapter 4: Scorched Aftermath] -> @[skill:40635:Chapter 2: Igniting Burst] -> @[skill:42898:Epilogue: Ashes of the Just] .
- When providing stability, make sure to use another skill to cover your stability. Covering your stability means applying boons right after using your stab skill so it is less likely to get stripped/corrupted.
![image](~img:1)

---

## Rotation:

- **Stability Skills:**
  - @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] 
  - Opening @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] when passive is active (inactive: @[skill:68650:Tome of Courage] ) through @[trait:612:Indomitable Courage] . 
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] -> @[skill:44455:Epilogue: Unbroken Lines] - Only provides one stack of stability but has long duration. Good to use when waiting for CDs or for your troubadour to reapply stability.
  - @[skill:43357:Mantra of Liberation]  (3 stacks - last stack is most powerful but should be reserved as a last resort).
- **Resistance Skills:**
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] 
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage]  -> @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand]  - Drops symbol that provides resistance at intervals.
- **Aegis Skills:**
  - @[skill:9084:"Advance!"]  - Two stacks
  - @[skill:15834:Shield of Judgment] 
  - @[skill:41714:Mantra of Solace]  - Two stacks
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage]  - Provides aegis periodically through passive.
- **Stunbreaks:** @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] (self), @[skill:43357:Mantra of Liberation] , opening @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] when passive is active, @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand] 
- Example Rotation:
  - First movement: @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] -> Open all tomes (buffs to bury stab) -> DPS Rotation
  - Second movement: @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] -> @[skill:44455:Epilogue: Unbroken Lines] -> @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand] -> Continue boon application and DPS.
  - Third movement: @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] -> Continue boon application and DPS. 
- Typical DPS Combo: 
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] -> @[skill:72978:Gleaming Disc] -> @[skill:73094:Solar Storm] -> @[skill:44364:Tome of Justice] -> @[skill:40015:Chapter 4: Scorched Aftermath] 
  - Rotate through weapon skills and provide aegis to proc @[skill:22499:Mystic Rebuke] for damage.
  - damage.
  - When in spear and have no other skills to use, spam @[skill:73055:Daybreaking Slash] .

---

## During Fight:

- Continuously refresh stability stacks to maintain stability throughout the fight. <u>Do not burn all your stab skills at once, space them out</u> - if done correctly, you and your Troubadour should be able to cover gaps between each other. Anything over 10 stacks of stab is a waste.
- Providing stability and aegis to party members also provides them with quickness via @[trait:2076:Stalwart Speed] . Apply aegis as much as possible - slightly spaced out to not waste aegis stacks.
- Use CC skills on wells calls such as @[skill:45402:Blazing Edge]  and @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] . Can also use CC to catch enemy tail and enemies without stability.
- Provide resistance during movement calls. 
- Provide stun breaks during movement calls/dodging enemy damage.

---

## Tips & Tricks:

- Provide resistance and quickness during initial bombs. This helps our DPS players from being blinded during their burst skills. Example of rotation during bomb: 
  - @[skill:40915:Mantra of Potence]  + @[skill:41714:Mantra of Solace]  for quickness
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] for resistance (and CC) at bomb location.
- Use @[skill:9091:Shield of Absorption]  when out of melee range to block enemy projectiles. Do not use it when we’re well bombing/bursting as this will push enemies away from our damage.
- You can open @[skill:41780:Tome of Resolve] and immediately close it for some self quickness.
- If there are heavy condis on party members, you can use @[skill:41780:Tome of Resolve]  -> @[skill:40679:Chapter 2: Radiant Recovery]  for additional cleanses. Do not use the other skills in this tome - they cost too many pages.
- When in heavy melee grind, you can stack your damage with Mystic Rebuke to get a nice spike off. Example rotation:
  - @[skill:44364:Tome of Justice] -> @[skill:40015:Chapter 4: Scorched Aftermath]  -> @[skill:40635:Chapter 2: Igniting Burst]  -> @[skill:41714:Mantra of Solace]  -> @[skill:9084:"Advance!"]  -> @[skill:15834:Shield of Judgment]  -> @[skill:40624:Symbol of Vengeance] 
- Niche: Spear skills @[skill:73094:Solar Storm]  and @[skill:72978:Gleaming Disc]  should only be used when under the Illuminated buff. Typically if you have @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance]  activated, you can press the two back to back. However, if you want to press them without Symbol of Luminance active, use @[skill:72940:Helio Rush]  to trigger buff.
  - @[skill:72940:Helio Rush]  is a movement skill, use with caution.

---

## Example Gameplay:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GwabSShkHM0

# **Berserker's Hammer guide**

--- 

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] will be your main opener to get you into combat and provides cripple on the enemy to help catch and keep the enemy locked down.
Can use @[skill:14404:Signet of Might] right before @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] to make it unblockable .
@[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] will give you full adrenaline bar to allow you to go into @[skill:30435:Berserk] 
dont use it if your not in combat. it will immediately drain and you will miss your window to go @[skill:30435:Berserk]
 
"Wells" calls is the ideal time to get things going


---

### Opening Burst Spike Rotation

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] - @[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

 or

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] - @[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

https://youtu.be/_H9Z40ztr7Y

---

### **Double F1 Combos** 
Can be done every 4 seconds

@[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

---

### **Triple F1 Combos** 
If your @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] comes back up while still in berserk use it for another triple f1 combo chain
you wont have much time here as its a signal you are approaching the end of @[skill:30435:Berserk] up time. Use it or lose it

@[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

---


### **Example full rotation if grinding on the enemy**
You want to stitch the above combo's together using your other skills and auto's to build adrenaline in between the 4 second combo windows

@[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:14554:Hundred Blades] - @[skill:14356:Greatsword Swing] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash]- @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]  - @[skill:14554:Hundred Blades] - @[skill:14510:Bladetrail] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]


---

### **Out of Berserk Hammer Combo**
@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14387:Earthshaker] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14511:Backbreaker] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---

### **Build Variations**

@[relic:100916:Relic of the Thief]   for a 5% hit damage increase
with a 5 second weapon swap use this rotation on Hammer for maximum damage 

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]


---



## **Additional Notes**


This is a **High APM** build to get the most DPS out of it.
Burst damage that creates downs comes from F1 combos 
Practice before you bring it to raid.

Don't @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] or @[skill:14387:Earthshaker] in front of tag. You will get stripped and killed. The AOE is large enough to land right behind tag on bombs
Try to follow up every @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] with a @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] to get the big bonus damage

Use @[skill:14419:Battle Standard] at any point in the rotation when opportunity is there

# Support Troubadour Guide

---

## General Description
**Role**: Primary Stab, Boons, Damage Mitigation
**Responsibilities**: Troubadours are the primary stability provider for the squad. Along with being the highest generation of stability, it provides boons, barriers, and healing to support the squad. It can also mitigate a large amount of incoming damage through blocks and invulns.
**Positioning**: You are one of the tankiest roles in the squad with a lot of survivability. Be sure to stack on the commander as much as you can to help mitigate damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:
- As one of the two stability sources in your party, we encourage you to talk with your Firebrand in your line to discuss who does the first source of stab.
- Generally, troubadours do not have anything to do during might or swiftness stacking.
- Provide stability (such as charging @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration]) at around 1200 range during engage and cover the stability. This means applying boons right after using your stab skill so it is less likely to get stripped/corrupted. Consider standing back while charging to prevent getting interrupted.
![image](~img:3)
- The first hit from an enemy zerg is typically the hardest. Using @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] on engage can help mitigate damage and give your druid breathing room to heal the squad up.

---

## Rotation:
### Stability Skills:
- @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration] - Cast time required
  - Charging mantra is a very strong stab. Unlike firebrands, troubs should use up all mantra stacks.
  - @[skill:10238:Power Break] - Instantaneous, 2 stacks.
- @[skill:76931:Crescendo] - Cast time required
- @[skill:76611:Tale of the Honorable Rogue] - Instantaneous
- All instrument skills (F1-F4) - Cast time required, very short duration (Self stab).
### Barrier Application:
- @[skill:76931:Crescendo] 
- @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] 
### Stability Rotation:
Stability Rotations vary from player to player. Below are examples of what it might look like. Note that you can weave additional weapon skills into the stability rotation not shown in the demo.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eqt1NVM5aNo

---

## During Fight:
- On wells calls, use @[skill:30643:Tides of Time] to try and stun enemy zerg on top of our wells.
- On august queen calls, channel @[skill:76971:Tale of the August Queen] prior to moving into the spot to reduce the chance of getting interrupted. Also take into consideration if you have quickness or not (i.e. if you have a harbinger in your party).
- <u>Do not burn all your stab skills at once, space them out.</u> If done correctly, you and your Firebrand should be able to cover gaps between each other. Anything over 10 stacks of stab is a waste.
- When using @[skill:30769:Echo of Memory] and @[skill:29649:Deja Vu] (Both Shield 4 stacks), <u>be up front with the commander if not further to get the most out of your blocks</u>. Being 300 range behind the commander while blocking is not effective. Some great opportunities to use them include but not limited to:
  - While we’re standing and grinding against enemy zerg at close range.
  - When making large movements like crossing tons of damage.
  - When the squad is shoving into enemy zerg for a spike.
- Cooldown for @[skill:30769:Echo of Memory] only starts after the buff expires or after using @[skill:29649:Deja Vu] ; this adds 10 seconds to the CD if the flip isn't used. Make sure to have an enemy targeted to spawn the phantasm.
- Casting @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] mitigates damage, make sure to walk out in front while casting it.

---

## Tips & Tricks:
- If we’re kiting/the enemy kited and you used @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration] and we happen to be out of combat, the cooldown can be reset/reduced by quickly swapping the skill on your skill bar. Pro tip: Right-clicking allows you to swap the skill.
- The last played instrument modifies @[skill:76931:Crescendo] via @[trait:2441:Altered Chord] , @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] (3 second daze) & @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] (Heal) are both very good; don’t delay @[skill:76931:Crescendo] or @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp]  for it though, manipulate it with your instrument order.
- Phantasms generate 2 notes due to @[trait:2374:Harmonize] , the first instantly and the second on a delay when the phantasm dies.
- Troubs have 5 CC skills (flute, drum, rifle 4, drum-empowered crescendo, shield 5). Make sure to use them while we clash.
- Do not double click @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] unless you need portal. The CD is increased.
- Using Notes Effectively:
  - @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] - Strong healing, should be used with 3 notes (3 note harp doubles the healing of 1 note harp).
  - @[skill:76552:Lively Lute] - Use it off CD with at least 2 notes to maintain buff.
  - @[skill:76746:Flustering Flute] & @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] - Use with 0 or 1 note. Note: Using it with 1 note helps Crescendo to recharge faster.
- Casting @[skill:76850:Tale of the Soulkeeper] or @[skill:76611:Tale of the Honorable Rogue] provides notes if @[skill:76552:Lively Lute] or @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] are playing, respectively. Consider this while going through your rotation.

---

## Example Gameplay:

https://youtu.be/zvQ4dRkcHgI?si=v-SZuUU938tcORch

# Wanderer's Firebrand Guide

---

## General Description
**Role**: Secondary Stab, Boons, Cleave
**Responsibilities**: Firebrands are the secondary stability provider for the squad. They provide various boons including aegis, resistance, swiftness, quickness, might, etc. and stunbreaks through their elite mantra.
**Positioning**: Stack on commander. With consistent aegis application and a decent amount of toughness (Wanderer’s), firebrands should be tanking a significant amount of incoming damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:

- Charge your mantras the moment you load in/respawn.
- As one of the two stability sources in your party, we encourage you to talk with your Troubadour in your line to discuss who does the first source of stab.
- Ensure your party has swiftness. Use @[skill:9084:"Advance!"]  It is the FB’s responsibility to provide swiftness pre-engage. 
- When commander calls for might stacks, use @[skill:44364:Tome of Justice] -> @[skill:40015:Chapter 4: Scorched Aftermath] -> @[skill:40635:Chapter 2: Igniting Burst] -> @[skill:42898:Epilogue: Ashes of the Just] .
- When providing stability, make sure to use another skill to cover your stability. Covering your stability means applying boons right after using your stab skill so it is less likely to get stripped/corrupted.
![image](~img:1)

---

## Rotation:

- **Stability Skills:**
  - @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] 
  - Opening @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] when passive is active (inactive: @[skill:68650:Tome of Courage] ) through @[trait:612:Indomitable Courage] . 
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] -> @[skill:44455:Epilogue: Unbroken Lines] - Only provides one stack of stability but has long duration. Good to use when waiting for CDs or for your troubadour to reapply stability.
  - @[skill:43357:Mantra of Liberation]  (3 stacks - last stack is most powerful but should be reserved as a last resort).
- **Resistance Skills:**
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] 
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage]  -> @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand]  - Drops symbol that provides resistance at intervals.
- **Aegis Skills:**
  - @[skill:9084:"Advance!"]  - Two stacks
  - @[skill:15834:Shield of Judgment] 
  - @[skill:41714:Mantra of Solace]  - Two stacks
  - @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage]  - Provides aegis periodically through passive.
- **Stunbreaks:** @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] (self), @[skill:43357:Mantra of Liberation] , opening @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] when passive is active, @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand] 
- Example Rotation:
  - First movement: @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] -> Open all tomes (buffs to bury stab) -> DPS Rotation
  - Second movement: @[skill:42259:Tome of Courage] -> @[skill:44455:Epilogue: Unbroken Lines] -> @[skill:40988:Chapter 4: Stalwart Stand] -> Continue boon application and DPS.
  - Third movement: @[skill:9153:"Stand Your Ground!"] -> Continue boon application and DPS. 
- Typical DPS Combo: 
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] -> @[skill:72978:Gleaming Disc] -> @[skill:73094:Solar Storm] -> @[skill:44364:Tome of Justice] -> @[skill:40015:Chapter 4: Scorched Aftermath] 
  - Rotate through weapon skills and provide aegis to proc @[skill:22499:Mystic Rebuke] for damage.
  - damage.
  - When in spear and have no other skills to use, spam @[skill:73055:Daybreaking Slash] .

---

## During Fight:

- Continuously refresh stability stacks to maintain stability throughout the fight. <u>Do not burn all your stab skills at once, space them out</u> - if done correctly, you and your Troubadour should be able to cover gaps between each other. Anything over 10 stacks of stab is a waste.
- Providing stability and aegis to party members also provides them with quickness via @[trait:2076:Stalwart Speed] . Apply aegis as much as possible - slightly spaced out to not waste aegis stacks.
- Use CC skills on wells calls such as @[skill:45402:Blazing Edge]  and @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] . Can also use CC to catch enemy tail and enemies without stability.
- Provide resistance during movement calls. 
- Provide stun breaks during movement calls/dodging enemy damage.

---

## Tips & Tricks:

- Provide resistance and quickness during initial bombs. This helps our DPS players from being blinded during their burst skills. Example of rotation during bomb: 
  - @[skill:40915:Mantra of Potence]  + @[skill:41714:Mantra of Solace]  for quickness
  - @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance] for resistance (and CC) at bomb location.
- Use @[skill:9091:Shield of Absorption]  when out of melee range to block enemy projectiles. Do not use it when we’re well bombing/bursting as this will push enemies away from our damage.
- You can open @[skill:41780:Tome of Resolve] and immediately close it for some self quickness.
- If there are heavy condis on party members, you can use @[skill:41780:Tome of Resolve]  -> @[skill:40679:Chapter 2: Radiant Recovery]  for additional cleanses. Do not use the other skills in this tome - they cost too many pages.
- When in heavy melee grind, you can stack your damage with Mystic Rebuke to get a nice spike off. Example rotation:

- Niche: Spear skills @[skill:73094:Solar Storm]  and @[skill:72978:Gleaming Disc]  should only be used when under the Illuminated buff. Typically if you have @[skill:73132:Symbol of Luminance]  activated, you can press the two back to back. However, if you want to press them without Symbol of Luminance active, use @[skill:72940:Helio Rush]  to trigger buff.
  - @[skill:72940:Helio Rush]  is a movement skill, use with caution.

build.notes:
# Heal Druid Guide

---

## General Description:
**Role**: Heals, Cleanse, Resurrect, Damage Mitigation
**Responsibilities**: Druids are the primary healer and cleanser. They provide resurrect utility through their elite skill and additional damage mitigation through their pets.
**Positioning**: Stay close to the center of stack. With full minstrels gear, you have high toughness and a pet that mitigates damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:
- When the commander calls for might stacks, use @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] and @[skill:71963:Oaken Cudgel] to blast fire fields for might.
- If you notice your party line does not have swiftness, you can also use @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] to provide swiftness.
- Right before melee engage/cross movement, cast @[skill:31700:Vine Surge] to catch enemy zerg with immob. You can also throw it into where the enemy zerg is veiling to catch stragglers.
- If people are getting caught with immobs during initial push, you can also cast @[skill:12497:Frost Spirit]  to provide early resistance.
- Enter @[skill:31869:Celestial Avatar] (CA) on clash.

---

## Rotation:
- When in CA, spam @[skill:31796:Cosmic Ray] to heal. **DO NOT USE @[skill:32253:Rejuvenating Tides]**. 
- Use @[skill:32242:Seed of Life] and @[skill:31318:Lunar Impact] for cleanses and @[relic:101268:Relic of Karakosa] proc for heals.
- Use @[skill:12489:Healing Spring] on initial melee clashes. It provides heals, cleanses, and regeneration.
- Outside of CA, rotation through your weapon skills to provide enough heals to return to CA. Skills to use include:
  - @[skill:31496:Sublime Conversion] + @[skill:31535:Ancestral Grace] - Provides a water field and blast finisher, lots of heals.
  - @[skill:12621:Call of the Wild] , @[skill:71963:Oaken Cudgel] (This is a movement skill, be careful when using), @[skill:71999:Flourish]
  - @[skill:12620:Hunter's Call] procs @[trait:964:Windborne Notes] , which provides regen
  - Rotating through the above should be more than enough to get back into CA.
- Back to spamming @[skill:31796:Cosmic Ray] 

---

## Tips & Tricks:
- Use @[skill:31700:Vine Surge]  on wells calls or when trying to catch enemy tail. Enemies without resistance will be caught with immob. 
- Cast @[skill:12569:Spirit of Nature]  on downed allies based on your party order or as directed by the commander.
  - <u>Note: there is a decent cast time on the skill so you will need to cast it preemptively.</u> Trying to use spirit on downed allies after they’ve been downed will likely result in them being cleaved out before they can get resurrected. This is especially true on the initial clash where the damage is the highest.
- Use pet skills on CD to proc @[trait:1055:Rejuvenation] . Swap pets when one's dead.

---

## Example Gameplay:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DIl7zPaQegA

http://youtube.com/watch?v=n9uRhY_mRl0&feature=youtu.be

build.notes:
# Support Troubadour Guide

---

## General Description
**Role**: Primary Stab, Boons, Damage Mitigation
**Responsibilities**: Troubadours are the primary stability provider for the squad. Along with being the highest generation of stability, it provides boons, barriers, and healing to support the squad. It can also mitigate a large amount of incoming damage through blocks and invulns.
**Positioning**: You are one of the tankiest roles in the squad with a lot of survivability. Be sure to stack on the commander as much as you can to help mitigate damage.

---

## Pre-Engage:
- As one of the two stability sources in your party, we encourage you to talk with your Firebrand in your line to discuss who does the first source of stab.
- Generally, troubadours do not have anything to do during might or swiftness stacking.
- Provide stability (such as charging @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration]) at around 1200 range during engage and cover the stability. This means applying boons right after using your stab skill so it is less likely to get stripped/corrupted. Consider standing back while charging to prevent getting interrupted.
![image](~img:3)
- The first hit from an enemy zerg is typically the hardest. Using @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] on engage can help mitigate damage and give your druid breathing room to heal the squad up.

---

## Rotation:
### Stability Skills:
- @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration] - Cast time required
  - Charging mantra is a very strong stab. Unlike firebrands, troubs should use up all mantra stacks.
  - @[skill:10238:Power Break] - Instantaneous, 2 stacks.
- @[skill:76931:Crescendo] - Cast time required
- @[skill:76611:Tale of the Honorable Rogue] - Instantaneous
- All instrument skills (F1-F4) - Cast time required, very short duration (Self stab).
### Barrier Application:
- @[skill:76931:Crescendo] 
- @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] 
### Stability Rotation:
Stability Rotations vary from player to player. Below are examples of what it might look like. Note that you can weave additional weapon skills into the stability rotation not shown in the demo.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eqt1NVM5aNo

---

## During Fight:
- On wells calls, use @[skill:30643:Tides of Time] to try and stun enemy zerg on top of our wells.
- On august queen calls, channel @[skill:76971:Tale of the August Queen] prior to moving into the spot to reduce the chance of getting interrupted. Also take into consideration if you have quickness or not (i.e. if you have a harbinger in your party).
- <u>Do not burn all your stab skills at once, space them out.</u> If done correctly, you and your Firebrand should be able to cover gaps between each other. Anything over 10 stacks of stab is a waste.
- When using @[skill:30769:Echo of Memory] and @[skill:29649:Deja Vu] (Both Shield 4 stacks), <u>be up front with the commander if not further to get the most out of your blocks</u>. Being 300 range behind the commander while blocking is not effective. Some great opportunities to use them include but not limited to:
  - While we’re standing and grinding against enemy zerg at close range.
  - When making large movements like crossing tons of damage.
  - When the squad is shoving into enemy zerg for a spike.
- Cooldown for @[skill:30769:Echo of Memory] only starts after the buff expires or after using @[skill:29649:Deja Vu] ; this adds 10 seconds to the CD if the flip isn't used. Make sure to have an enemy targeted to spawn the phantasm.
- Casting @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] mitigates damage, make sure to walk out in front while casting it.

---

## Tips & Tricks:
- If we’re kiting/the enemy kited and you used @[skill:10237:Mantra of Concentration] and we happen to be out of combat, the cooldown can be reset/reduced by quickly swapping the skill on your skill bar. Pro tip: Right-clicking allows you to swap the skill.
- The last played instrument modifies @[skill:76931:Crescendo] via @[trait:2441:Altered Chord] , @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] (3 second daze) & @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] (Heal) are both very good; don’t delay @[skill:76931:Crescendo] or @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp]  for it though, manipulate it with your instrument order.
- Phantasms generate 2 notes due to @[trait:2374:Harmonize] , the first instantly and the second on a delay when the phantasm dies.
- Troubs have 5 CC skills (flute, drum, rifle 4, drum-empowered crescendo, shield 5). Make sure to use them while we clash.
- Do not double click @[skill:72008:Singularity Shot] unless you need portal. The CD is increased.
- Using Notes Effectively:
  - @[skill:77077:Harmonious Harp] - Strong healing, should be used with 3 notes (3 note harp doubles the healing of 1 note harp).
  - @[skill:76552:Lively Lute] - Use it off CD with at least 2 notes to maintain buff.
  - @[skill:76746:Flustering Flute] & @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] - Use with 0 or 1 note. Note: Using it with 1 note helps Crescendo to recharge faster.
- Casting @[skill:76850:Tale of the Soulkeeper] or @[skill:76611:Tale of the Honorable Rogue] provides notes if @[skill:76552:Lively Lute] or @[skill:77079:Deafening Drum] are playing, respectively. Consider this while going through your rotation.

---

## Example Gameplay:

https://youtu.be/zvQ4dRkcHgI?si=v-SZuUU938tcORch

# **Berserker's Hammer guide**

--- 

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] will be your main opener to get you into combat and provides cripple on the enemy to help catch and keep the enemy locked down.
Can use @[skill:14404:Signet of Might] right before @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] to make it unblockable .
@[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] will give you full adrenaline bar to allow you to go into @[skill:30435:Berserk] 
dont use it if your not in combat. it will immediately drain and you will miss your window to go @[skill:30435:Berserk]
 
"Wells" calls is the ideal time to get things going


---

### Opening Burst Spike Rotation

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] - @[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

 or

@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14410:Signet of Fury] - @[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

https://youtu.be/_H9Z40ztr7Y

---

### **Double F1 Combos** 
Can be done every 4 seconds

@[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

---

### **Triple F1 Combos** 
If your @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] comes back up while still in berserk use it for another triple f1 combo chain
you wont have much time here as its a signal you are approaching the end of @[skill:30435:Berserk] up time. Use it or lose it

@[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]

---


### **Example full rotation if grinding on the enemy**
You want to stitch the above combo's together using your other skills and auto's to build adrenaline in between the 4 second combo windows

@[skill:30435:Berserk] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:30189:Blood Reckoning] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:14554:Hundred Blades] - @[skill:14356:Greatsword Swing] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash]- @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]  - @[skill:14554:Hundred Blades] - @[skill:14510:Bladetrail] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]


---

### **Out of Berserk Hammer Combo**
@[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:14387:Earthshaker] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14511:Backbreaker] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow]

---

### **Build Variations**

@[relic:100916:Relic of the Thief]   for a 5% hit damage increase
with a 5 second weapon swap use this rotation on Hammer for maximum damage 

---


@[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:14482:Hammer Shock] - @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] - @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] - @[skill:999999:Weapon Swap] - @[skill:29852:Arc Divider]


---



## **Additional Notes**


This is a **High APM** build to get the most DPS out of it.
Burst damage that creates downs comes from F1 combos 
Practice before you bring it to raid.

Don't @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] or @[skill:14387:Earthshaker] in front of tag. You will get stripped and killed. The AOE is large enough to land right behind tag on bombs
Try to follow up every @[skill:30879:Rupturing Smash] with a @[skill:14386:Fierce Blow] to get the big bonus damage

Use @[skill:14419:Battle Standard] at any point in the rotation when opportunity is there


---
 
### **Skills to avoid while in combat**

@[skill:14446:Rush]

turns.agentText:
# EWW Raid Report — June 8, 2026
### Commander: Prez Ashton · Squad: 30 · Opponents: ~54 avg.

---

## Result: 20 Wins / 7 Losses (74% Win Rate)

Fighting outnumbered nearly 2-to-1, Prez's squad held a strong **2.28 KDR** — 395 enemy deaths to 173 squad deaths. A genuinely solid night overall.

---

## Top Performers

| Category | Player | Value |
|---|---|---|
| Damage | FatherHyperion (Tempest) | 10.6M |
| Healing | Arx (Druid) | 6.06M |
| Cleanses | Arx (Druid) | 3,543 |
| Strips | MaximDeathgun (Reaper) | 1,939 |

Arx stood out as the squad's most complete support — top healing **and** top cleanses while dying only twice across 27 fights.

---

## Areas for Improvement

### 1 · Troubadour Positioning — hotep is a clear outlier
Troubadour is the tankiest support role in the comp. Your own guide says to *"stack on commander as much as you can."* The contrast within the same role on the same night tells the story:

| Troubadour | Deaths | Downs |
|---|---|---|
| Beornin | 3 | 3 |
| Oceanomega | 3 | 10 |
| WindNature | 4 | 8 |
| moxic | 4 | 7 |
| Yesmad | 5 | 11 |
| **hotep** | **5** | **19** |

Beornin ran the same build and posted only 3 downs all night. hotep went down 19 times. This is not a build issue — it's a positioning issue specific to this player and warrants a direct conversation.

---

### 2 · Firebrand Cleanses — Tome of Resolve underused
The FB guide explicitly calls for opening **Tome of Resolve → Chapter 2: Radiant Recovery** during heavy condition waves. In a 30v54 fight, that's exactly when it's needed most. Several FBs appear not to be using it:

| Firebrand | Cleanses |
|---|---|
| Subvert | 689 |
| Ashtonlightstone | 474 |
| undiee | 309 |
| Vettexl | 300 |
| **sbr** | **129** |

sbr (129) and Vettexl (300) are well below expectations. The Druid core should not be carrying the squad's entire cleanse burden — Firebrands need to actively contribute during condition-heavy engagements.

---

### 3 · Heal Druid Survivability — IronOre vs. Arx
Both players ran the same Minstrel's Druid build. The survival gap is too wide to be anything other than positioning:

| Druid | Healing | Cleanses | Deaths | Downs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Arx** | 6.06M | 3,543 | 2 | 4 |
| **IronOre** | 5.81M | 3,246 | 9 | 9 |

Losing a primary healer on 9 of 27 fights puts constant pressure on the rest of the support core. The guide is clear: stay center of stack. IronOre's death total in full Minstrel's gear strongly suggests they are not doing this consistently.

---

### 4 · Bouzouki (Specter) — Most Damaging Survivability Failure
**10 deaths · 20 downs** — the worst numbers on the squad, in a support/barrier role. Every death is a window where their entire party loses barrier coverage. With LightspeedJack (also Specter) posting just 4 deaths and similar healing output, this is again a player-specific positioning issue rather than a role problem.

---

### 5 · Stability Chain Coordination — Squad-Wide Coaching Point
The Troubadour guide stresses: *"Do not burn all your stab skills at once — you and your Firebrand should cover gaps between each other."* The squad's **365 total downs across 27 fights** (~13.5 per fight) points to moments where stability coverage collapses simultaneously across a party rather than being staggered. When both the Troubadour and FB in a party spend their stab at the same time, the next spike sends the whole party to the ground at once. This is worth reinforcing in the pre-raid talk before next session.

---

## Summary

| Area | Priority | Who |
|---|---|---|
| hotep positioning | 🔴 High | hotep |
| Bouzouki positioning | 🔴 High | Bouzouki |
| IronOre positioning | 🟡 Medium | IronOre |
| FB Tome of Resolve usage | 🟡 Medium | sbr, Vettexl, undiee |
| Stab chain coordination | 🟡 Medium | Squad-wide |

The foundation is strong — Prez is winning the fights that matter at an outnumbered disadvantage. Fixing survivability on the support roles is the clearest path to pushing that win rate even higher.
